_help: Portable safety + behavior contract for the self-prompting loop. Both runners (loop.py and ralph_loop.sh) read this. Keys starting with _ are comments and ignored. Fill thresholds with real values for your product; keep this in sync with CLAUDE.md section 8.
run_goal: Produce one validated output for <unit of work> that passes the quality and safety gates, fully explained.
_trigger_help: What kicks off a run. 'manual' = a human starts it; 'cron' = on a schedule (set cron); 'event' = an external event/webhook (set event). The loop is a trigger + a decision-maker — the trigger is the heartbeat, the model is the decision.
_concurrency_help: Prevent collisions. singleton_key = run at most ONE instance per this key (e.g. per ticker/service/screen) so a scheduled run can't stomp a running one. Empty = no limit. A durable engine enforces this natively; the portable runner uses a lockfile.
_worktrees_help: Optional parallel worker mode. Each worker writes in an isolated Git worktree; the coordinator owns integration and merge. Keep max_workers small because isolation removes races but does not make model calls free.
_hard_limits_help: Absolute, judgment-free brakes. Checked every cycle by BOTH runners. per_run caps the whole run; per_cycle stops the loop if a single cycle blows its budget (a runaway signal). gate_failure_streak trips a hard stop after N consecutive gate failures (thrashing).
_context_budget_help: Cheap-mode context governor. orbit-context doctor reads these thresholds before expensive loops; orbit-context compact archives old append-only logs, keeping the last N events. Token estimates are deliberately rough (bytes / token_estimate_divisor) and used as a stoplight, not billing truth.
_eval_gates_help: Proceed only if all pass. A single failure routes a fix task into the queue; gate_failure_streak consecutive failures trips a hard stop (the loop is thrashing). Thresholds are domain-specific; fit them to your product.
_approval_checkpoints_help: Values: 'auto' = loop may do it; 'human' = pause and wait for approval; 'FORBIDDEN' = never. Numeric value = threshold above which a human is required. Add the side effects your product can take.
_gears_help: The Gearbox (.orbit/skills/loop-tiers.md). Cheap mode is the default: the ORCHESTRATOR sizes the smallest gear that proves the result, runs orbit-context doctor before T2+, and asks before any wider fan-out. 'deep' caps T3 dynamic fan-out; 'mission' flags T4 durability. Consumed by the model at sizing time, not by the runners.
_cost_mode_help: Default behavior that prevents Orbit from becoming a token amplifier. The model obeys these before spawning agents; scripts/orbit-context is the mechanical stoplight for local context bloat.
_independent_qa_help: Optional second-provider release gate. The Planner arms an acceptance manifest before implementation; after the internal gates pass, Orbit reviews only an exact committed snapshot. Both enabled and external_export.approved must be true before any private code is sent. Project-specific rubrics stay in the project and are listed by each request.
_model_policy_help: Two-lane model switching. The everyday executor runs the loop cheaply; the Advisor is an on-demand Opus judgment call, not a default worker. Claude Code subagents use aliases ('sonnet'/'opus') so the host resolves to the current account model family; display names preserve the intended product language.

executor.purpose: Plan/build/verify ordinary loop work under Cost Mode Lite.

done_when: STATE.md run_goal satisfied AND internal Safety/Reviewer/QA gates passed AND the independent QA gate passed when enabled
_proof_help: First-class verification: the CONCRETE evidence a cycle actually worked, not just 'looks done'. kind: command (run it, expect exit 0) | metric (read a number, compare) | checklist (all items true) | review (Reviewer rubric >= threshold). The loop must produce this proof before calling a task done.
_dispatch_help: The model seam. model_policy declares the executor/advisor lanes; dispatch() should choose the configured model for the role it is running. orchestrator names the backend wired into loop.py dispatch(); model is the legacy single-model fallback.
_paths_help: Where the loop reads/writes. Relative to repo root.
